chemistry.scope_summary: Benchmark photochemical reaction used to test the luminescent solar concentrator-photomicroreactor (LSC-PM) device performance

optimization_table.key_findings: 200 ppm LSC-PM showed highest conversion, 4.5-fold acceleration compared to non-doped reactor

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Working principle of the LSC-PM. A) Comparison and analogy of solar harvesting and photon transfer in a leaf and in a LSC-PM. In the leaf photosystem, the energy of the photons harvested by the antenna pigment molecules is transferred to the reaction center where it eventually reaches the primary electron acceptor. Analogously, in the LSC-PM, the photons absorbed by the embedded fluores

figures.description: Comparison of photocatalytic conversion performance under different light sources (Blue LEDs vs Solar Simulator) and doping concentrations (Non-doped, 10 ppm, 50 ppm, 100 ppm, 200 ppm). Panel A shows conversion vs light input power for Blue LEDs, while panels B and C show conversion vs residence time for Solar Simulator under different experimental configurations.
figures.caption: Figure 2.  Edge emission measurements. A) Experimental setup for the measurement of edge emissions. B) Edge emissions of the LSC-PM with the channels filled with MB solutions of varying concentration, the percentage refers to the total incident. Figure 4.  Solar light outdoor experiment. A–C) 30-minute snapshots of the performance of a 200 ppm LR305 LSC-PM and a non-doped reactor on a partly sunny summer day with scattered cloud cover at different residence times (A 20 s, B 15 s, C 10 s). The average conversion in the 30- minute timeframe is reported along with the standard deviation. The LSC-PM is not only more efficient in gathering sol

translation_logic.flow_advantage: LSC-PM enables energy-efficient continuous-flow photochemistry using solar light with 4.5-fold acceleration compared to non-doped reactor
translation_logic.what_changed: Implementation of luminescent solar concentrator with photomicroreactor for enhanced light harvesting and photon transfer
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow processing under milder conditions

figure_descriptions: Working principle of LSC-PM with leaf analogy
Performance comparison under different light sources and doping concentrations
Edge emission measurements and experimental setup

conflicts: Light source wavelength reported as 654 nm in pass2 but described as blue LEDs in pass3
notes: Paper focuses on novel LSC-PM device rather than traditional batch-to-flow comparison. No batch baseline data provided. Key innovation is the luminescent solar concentrator integrated with microreactor for enhanced photon harvesting efficiency.
